# xing.report

> Free, live border-crossing wait times for the Detroit–Windsor corridor. It answers one question: which bridge do I take right now? Built for truck drivers, dispatchers, and everyday travelers.

## What it is
- Live wait times for two Detroit–Windsor crossings, compared side by side, in both directions (Detroit → Windsor and Windsor → Detroit), for trucks (commercial lanes) and cars (passenger lanes).
- Updated every 5 minutes. If a reading is more than 10 minutes old, the site says so rather than showing a stale number.
- No accounts, no ads, no cookies, no cross-site tracking — privacy-first, anonymous analytics only.

## Crossings
- Ambassador Bridge (Detroit–Windsor) — trucks and cars.
- Detroit–Windsor Tunnel — cars, plus low-profile trucks only (max 12 ft 8 in tall / 8 ft 6 in wide, no hazmat placards). Standard 13 ft 6 in trailers can't clear it, so it is never named the fastest freight crossing.
- Blue Water Bridge (Port Huron–Sarnia, ~60 miles north) — shown as a diversion option.
- Gordie Howe International Bridge — not open yet; the site tracks its status and shows it the day it opens.

## Data sources (all free, public, government)
- U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) — wait times into the U.S.
- Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) — wait times into Canada.
- Ontario 511 and MDOT Mi Drive — road incidents and approach cameras.
- U.S. National Weather Service and Environment and Climate Change Canada — weather alerts.
- Bank of Canada — daily exchange rate for tolls.

## Historical data
The site keeps a permanent archive of every reading and shows typical wait by hour ("best times to cross") once enough history has accumulated.

## Public data endpoints (JSON)
- https://xing.report/api/now — current wait times, alerts, tolls for all crossings.
- https://xing.report/api/patterns — typical wait by hour of day, from the archive.
- https://xing.report/openapi.json — OpenAPI 3 description of both endpoints (field names, meanings, response shapes).
- These endpoints are CORS-open (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *), so they can be fetched directly from a browser, tool, or agent.

## More detail for AI
- https://xing.report/llms-full.txt — the complete fact base in one file: every crossing's tolls, size limits, hazmat rules, hours, and status, with the date each was last verified and its official source.

## License and attribution
- Content and data are free to use, including by AI answer engines, under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0): https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
- Please attribute as: "xing.report (https://xing.report)".
- The underlying wait-time readings originate from U.S. CBP and Canada's CBSA, which are public government sources; xing.report collects, timestamps, archives, and presents them.
